title: Cannot declare static variable with same name in different blocks of method
body:
I have the following code:

```
// file: test.sv

module test;

  function do_stuff();
    begin: block0
      static int some_int;
    end
    begin: block1
      static int some_int;
    end
  endfunction 

endmodule
```

When I compile with `verilator --binary test.sv` I get:

```
%Error: test.sv:8:18: Duplicate declaration of signal: 'test.do_stuff__Vstatic__some_int'
    8 |       static int some_int;
      |                  ^~~~~~~~
        test.sv:5:18: ... Location of original declaration
    5 |       static int some_int;
      |                  ^~~~~~~~
%Error: Exiting due to 1 error(s)
```

It seems that `some_int` is put under `do_stuff` directly, not under `do_stuff.block0` and `do_stuff.block`.
